headline: EigenCloud's on-chain verifiability cannot be assessed: no deployed contract addresses found, and audit page link unresolvable this run

findings.text: No deployed contract addresses were provided in the address_book (null) and no contracts page could be resolved at https://www.eigencloud.xyz or https://docs.eigencloud.xyz during this run. Without at least one 0x address, bytecode verification on Etherscan cannot be checked.

The pinned GitHub org is 'Layr-Labs', which is the EigenLayer team. EigenCloud appears to be a product or frontend layer on top of EigenLayer. The canonical eigenlayer-contracts repo exists at https://github.com/Layr-Labs/eigenlayer-contracts, but it is unclear which specific commit or version corresponds to EigenCloud's currently-deployed contracts, and no deployment manifest was found this run.

The audit link https://docs.eigencloud.xyz/eigenlayer/security/audits resolves to EigenLayer's security/audits documentation (the domain prefix 'eigencloud.xyz' fronts EigenLayer docs). This page lists multiple audit engagements from Sigma Prime, Trail of Bits, and others, but this run could not confirm which specific contracts or commit hashes were in scope for the most recent audit versus what is currently deployed.

EigenLayer (Layr-Labs) has historically engaged Trail of Bits and Sigma Prime — both broadly recognized firms — but without resolving the specific audit report URLs and confirming their scope against deployed contracts, a V4 pass cannot be confirmed this run.
Post-audit drift cannot be assessed: no deployed contract addresses were identified, and therefore no comparison between the audited commit and the current on-chain bytecode could be performed.
Whether any deployed contracts use upgradeable proxies, and whether both proxy and implementation are verified on Etherscan, cannot be determined without knowing the deployed addresses.

rationale.verdict: Grading unknown because the address_book is null and no deployed contract addresses for EigenCloud could be located from the available sources during this run. Without at least one on-chain address, V1 (bytecode verification), V5 (post-audit drift), and V6 (proxy vs implementation verification) are all unresolvable. EigenCloud's relationship to EigenLayer is also ambiguous — it may be a product layer rather than a distinct set of contracts — which further blocks a confident verifiability assessment.

evidence.shows: Primary EigenLayer smart-contracts repository under the Layr-Labs GitHub org (the pinned github for EigenCloud). Confirms public repo exists but does not by itself confirm which contracts are deployed as EigenCloud.
EigenCloud docs audit page (mirrors EigenLayer security docs); lists audit engagements but specific contract addresses and commit SHAs in scope were not resolvable this run.
EigenCloud marketing/landing page; no deployed contract addresses or on-chain deployment manifest located here this run.

unknowns: V1: No deployed contract addresses found for EigenCloud on Ethereum; bytecode verification on Etherscan could not be checked.
V2: No deployment manifest or tagged release mapping EigenCloud's production deployment to a specific Layr-Labs/eigenlayer-contracts commit was found.
V3: Audit page URL was cited but specific auditor names, report PDFs, and in-scope commit SHAs could not be confirmed from the docs page this run.
V4: Auditor identity (firm names) could not be confirmed from a directly-fetched audit report this run; known from training data only.
V5: Post-audit drift cannot be assessed without deployed addresses and a confirmed audit commit.
V6: Proxy/implementation verification status unknown without deployed addresses.

protocol_metadata.about: EigenCloud is a product or infrastructure layer built on EigenLayer, the Ethereum restaking protocol developed by Layr-Labs. EigenLayer allows ETH stakers to extend cryptoeconomic security to additional services (Actively Validated Services, or AVSs) by restaking their staked ETH or liquid staking tokens. EigenCloud appears to package or present EigenLayer's restaking infrastructure — its exact differentiation from base EigenLayer, and whether it deploys its own distinct contracts, could not be confirmed from available sources this run.
